tool_input.prompt:
I need you to add `data-i18n` attributes to specific HTML elements in `/Users/Johnsonpampers/samus/projects/vnzla-oil/public/dashboard.html`. The i18n keys are already defined in the JS. You just need to add the attributes to the HTML elements. Here are the exact edits needed: 1. The historical chart title spans already have `data-i18n="subtitle"` etc. Find and tag these: - `Historical Production & 2026 Scenarios` → add `data-i18n="hist-title"` - `Prod & Scenarios` → add `data-i18n="hist-title-m"` 2. Panel titles (find the exact `` elements): - `All Producers` → add `data-i18n="all-producers"` - `Export Destinations (2025 Avg)` → add `data-i18n="export-dest"` - `Output by Country of Origin + Upside` → add `data-i18n="country-output"` - `Empresas Mixtas — JV Output (K bpd)` → add `data-i18n="empresas-mixtas"` (Note: this one is inside `Empresas Mixtas...`) - `Infrastructure Utilization` → add `data-i18n="infra-util"` - `Financial Overview` → add `data-i18n="financial"` - `Critical Context` → add `data-i18n="critical"` - `Infrastructure Map` → add `data-i18n="infra-map"` 3. Sidebar titles (`
`): - `Sanctions & Policy Timeline` → add `data-i18n="sb-timeline"` - `GL-50A — "Big Six"` → add `data-i18n="sb-gl50a"` - `Active Deals (Q1 2026)` → add `data-i18n="sb-deals"` - `Debt Exposure` → add `data-i18n="sb-debt"` 4. Scenario tags (the `
` elements): - `Bear — Sanctions Hold` → add `data-i18n="sc-bear"` - `Base — GL-50A Ramp` → add `data-i18n="sc-base"` - `Bull — Full Reopening` → add `data-i18n="sc-bull"` 5. Context headings (the `` inside critical context): - `POST-MADURO` → add `data-i18n="ctx-maduro"` - `REVENUE CONTROL` → add `data-i18n="ctx-revenue"` - `HORMUZ CRISIS` → add `data-i18n="ctx-hormuz"` - `OPEC STATUS` → add `data-i18n="ctx-opec"` 6. The Projections button text: - Find `Projections` text inside the projToggle button → wrap in `Projections` 7. Footer "View All Sources": - Find `View All Sources` → add `data-i18n="view-sources"` Use the Edit tool for each change. Read the file first to find exact strings. Make sure each edit is unique (include surrounding context if needed).

tool_input.prompt:
Research ALL documented oil spills in Venezuela from January 2025 to March 2026. I need:

For each spill:
1. Date (as precise as possible)
2. Location (specific place name, state, body of water)
3. Latitude/longitude (approximate if exact not available — use the named location to estimate)
4. Cause (pipeline leak, refinery incident, tanker, well blowout, etc.)
5. Volume/dimension (barrels spilled, area affected in km², shoreline km impacted)
6. Operator responsible (PDVSA, specific JV, etc.)
7. Status (cleaned up, ongoing, uncontained)
8. Environmental impact (wildlife, fishing, water supply, mangroves, etc.)
9. Source URL

Search for:
- PDVSA oil spills 2025 2026
- Venezuela crude oil leak pipeline
- Lake Maracaibo oil spill
- Paraguana refinery spill
- Morrocoy oil spill
- El Palito refinery leak
- Venezuela offshore oil spill
- Orinoco oil contamination
- Venezuelan coast oil slick

Venezuela has chronic spill problems — Lake Maracaibo alone has frequent pipeline leaks. There should be many incidents. Check environmental organizations (SOS Orinoco, local Venezuelan news, Reuters, Bloomberg, maritime tracking).

Return structured data with coordinates for each spill.

tool_input.args: ensure projects/vnzla-oil exists and is properly structured for oil spill research data

{
  "metadata": {

All figures sourced from NGOs (Clima 21, Observatorio de Ecología Política), local Venezuelan press (La Patilla, El Nacional, TalCual), and international wire services (Reuters, Bloomberg). Actual incident count for full period likely 30+; confirmed discrete events documented here: 13. NGO Clima 21 counted 13 spills/gas leaks/explosions in 2025 through June 2025 alone.",
    "primary_sources": [
      "https://pdvsa-adhoc.com/en/2025/06/oil-spills-in-venezuela/",

git add public/dashboard.html && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Add oil spill markers to infrastructure map (9 incidents, 2025-2026)

- 9 documented oil spills from Jan 2025 to Mar 2026 with coordinates
- Red pulsing markers with thick border for visibility
- Click popups show: date, cause, volume, area, status, impact, source
- New "Spills" filter tab (red) in map controls
- "All" view shows spills alongside infrastructure
- Largest: LOL-3 Gulf of Coro (~90K bbl), J-20 Morichal (~50K bbl),
  El Palito/Golfo Triste (225 km² slick)
- Most recent: two LOL pipeline failures Mar 25-26, 2026
- Sources: Reuters, La Patilla, Maritime Executive, Atmosi, OilPrice

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git push origin main && npx vercel --prod --yes 2>&1 | tail -2

curl -s https://vnzla-oil.vercel.app/api/prices | node -e "const c=[];process.stdin.on('data',d=>c.push(d));process.stdin.on('end',()=>{const d=JSON.parse(Buffer.concat(c));console.log('Brent:',d.brent?.price,'WTI:',d.wti?.price,'Merey:',d.merey?.price,'(spread:',d.merey?.spread+')','Petcoke:',d.petcoke?.price,d.petcoke?.live?'LIVE':'EST');})"

curl -s https://vnzla-oil.vercel.app/api/production | node -e "const c=[];process.stdin.on('data',d=>c.push(d));process.stdin.on('end',()=>{const d=JSON.parse(Buffer.concat(c));console.log('Source:',d.source,'Latest:',d.data?.[0]?.period,d.data?.[0]?.value,'TBPD','Count:',d.data?.length,'months');})"

tool_input.args:
create daily-vnzla-intel "0 13 * * *" --description "Daily Venezuela oil intelligence sweep — checks OFAC sanctions changes, oil spills, company news/deals, production updates. Updates dashboard data and pushes to GitHub." --prompt "You are a Venezuela oil intelligence agent. Your job is to check for updates to the VNZLA OIL dashboard at /Users/Johnsonpampers/samus/projects/vnzla-oil/public/dashboard.html.

## What to check daily:

### 1. OFAC Sanctions Changes
- Fetch https://ofac.treasury.gov/recent-actions and check for any Venezuela-related actions since yesterday
- Check for new General Licenses, amendments, or revocations
- If changes found: update the sidebar timeline in dashboard.html, update company OFAC status in the CO array, update the ticker

### 2. Oil Spills
- Search Google News RSS for 'Venezuela oil spill PDVSA pipeline leak' from the last 24 hours
- Search for 'derrame petroleo Venezuela PDVSA' (Spanish sources)
- If new spills found: add to spillData array in dashboard.html with coordinates, cause, volume, impact

### 3. Company News & Deals
- Search for news about: Chevron Venezuela, Shell Venezuela Dragon, NABEP Venezuela, Repsol Venezuela, ENI Venezuela, CNPC Venezuela
- Check for production updates, new deals, license changes
- If material changes: update the CO array entries (production numbers, status, detail text)

### 4. Production Data
- Check if OPEC Monthly Oil Market Report has new Venezuela data
- Check Trading Economics Venezuela crude oil production for updates
- If new monthly figure available: update the dataA/dataQ/dataM arrays in dashboard.html

### 5. Price Spread Check
- Verify the Merey-Brent spread is still ~$9. Check recent Merey pricing reports.
- If spread has materially changed, note it for MEREY_SPREAD env var update

## How to update:
1. Read the dashboard HTML file
2. Make targeted edits to the JavaScript data arrays
3. Commit with descriptive message
4. Push to GitHub (Vercel auto-deploys)

## Rules:
- Only update if you find CONFIRMED new information with sources
- Don't make changes based on speculation
- If nothing new found, just log 'No updates — all data current' and exit
- Keep the commit messages descriptive of what changed and why
- Always include source attribution in any data you add"

tool_input.query: select:RemoteTrigger

message.content:
You are a Venezuela oil intelligence agent. Run a daily sweep and update the VNZLA OIL dashboard with any new information.

The dashboard is at `public/dashboard.html`. All data lives in inline JavaScript arrays inside this file.

## DAILY CHECKS:

### 1. OFAC Sanctions Changes
- WebFetch https://ofac.treasury.gov/recent-actions and check for Venezuela-related actions in the last 24 hours
- Look for new General Licenses, amendments, revocations
- If found: update the sidebar timeline HTML (add new entry at top), update company OFAC status in the `CO` array, update ticker if needed

### 2. Oil Spills
- WebSearch for 'Venezuela oil spill PDVSA pipeline leak 2026'
- WebSearch for 'derrame petroleo Venezuela PDVSA 2026'
- If new spills found: add to the `spillData` array with lat/lon coordinates, date, cause, volume, impact, source

### 3. Company News & Deals
- WebSearch for each: 'Chevron Venezuela 2026', 'Shell Venezuela Dragon gas', 'NABEP Venezuela oil', 'Repsol Venezuela', 'ENI Venezuela oil', 'CNPC Venezuela oil'
- Look for: production updates, new deals signed, license changes, operational status
- If material changes: update the relevant entry in the `CO` array (cur, curL, tgt, detail, stL)

### 4. Production Data
- WebSearch for 'Venezuela crude oil production latest OPEC'
- WebSearch for 'PDVSA production barrels per day 2026'
- If a new monthly production figure is available that differs from what's in the dashboard: update the `dataM` and `dataQ` arrays, update `TOT` constant, update KPI HTML

### 5. Merey Spread
- WebSearch for 'Venezuela Merey crude oil price discount spread Brent'
- If the spread has materially changed from $9 (e.g., now $6 or $12), note it in the commit message

## HOW TO UPDATE:
1. Read `public/dashboard.html` — understand the current data
2. Use Edit tool for targeted changes to the JS data arrays
3. Commit with a descriptive message listing what changed and sources
4. Push to origin main — Vercel auto-deploys

## RULES:
- Only update with CONFIRMED new information from credible sources
- Never speculate or estimate — use reported figures only
- If nothing new found today, commit nothing. Just output 'No updates — all data current as of [date]'
- Include source URLs in commit messages
- Don't touch CSS, layout, or non-data code
- Don't break the JavaScript — test that arrays are valid JSON-like syntax after edits

tool_input.prompt:
Find the most recent Venezuela crude oil production figures for January, February, and March 2026. Check:
1. OPEC Monthly Oil Market Report (MOMR) - secondary sources
2. Trading Economics Venezuela crude oil production
3. CEIC Data
4. S&P Global / Kpler vessel tracking estimates
5. Any Reuters/Bloomberg reports with specific monthly figures

Return the most recent month available and the figure in thousand barrels per day. Be specific about the source and date of each figure. Keep response under 200 words.

tool_input.query: Venezuela crude oil production January February March 2026 OPEC
Venezuela crude oil production 2026 Trading Economics CEIC
Venezuela oil production February 2026 S&P Global Kpler estimates
"Venezuela" "crude oil production" "March 2026" OR "February 2026" thousand barrels

tool_input.command:
git add -A && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Production API: merge EIA + recent estimates for latest data

EIA lags ~3 months (latest: Dec 2025). Now supplements with
recent CEIC/Trading Economics/Kpler estimates for Jan-Mar 2026:
- Mar 2026: 1,090K (Kpler vessel exports)
- Feb 2026: 903K (CEIC crude-only)
- Jan 2026: 924K (Trading Economics)

API merges both sources: recent estimates fill the gap, EIA
provides the historical backbone. KPI shows per-point source.
Daily intel agent updates RECENT_ESTIMATES array as new data drops.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git push origin main && npx vercel --prod --yes 2>&1 | tail -2

sleep 3 && curl -s https://vnzla-oil.vercel.app/api/production | python3 -c "import sys,json;d=json.load(sys.stdin);print('Source:',d['source']);[print(f'  {r[\"period\"]}  {r[\"value\"]} TBPD  ({r.get(\"source\",\"\")})')for r in d['data'][:6]]"

git add -A && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Wire live production data to historical chart

/api/production data now feeds both the KPI and the chart:
- Monthly data array (dataM) updated with live values per period
- Quarterly data (dataQ) updated with latest month in each quarter
- Annual data (dataA) updated with latest month for each year
- Chart rebuilds after injection via buildChart()

The chart now visually reflects EIA + recent estimates (Mar 2026:
1,090K from Kpler) instead of showing stale hardcoded values.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git push origin main && npx vercel --prod --yes 2>&1 | tail -2

git add -A && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Chart: three production lines (EIA official, live estimate, blended)

- Blue solid: EIA Official (3-month lag, government data)
- Green dashed: Live Estimate (CEIC/Trading Economics/Kpler)
- Amber filled: Blended average of both (or whichever is available)
- Red dashed: Scenario projection (toggleable)

Each line independently sourced from /api/production response.
Where only one source exists for a period, blended = that source.
Where both exist, blended = average. Tooltips show period-over-period
change for all three lines.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git push origin main && npx vercel --prod --yes 2>&1 | tail -2

git add -A && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Fix chart: use hardcoded data as baseline, overlay live EIA + estimates

Before live data loads: all three lines show the hardcoded historical
data so the chart is fully populated 2015-2026.

After live data loads:
- EIA line: hardcoded baseline overwritten with live EIA where available
- Estimate line: only appears where CEIC/TE/Kpler estimates exist (recent)
- Blended: average of both where they overlap, else best available
- Scenario projection anchors from the blended line

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git push origin main && npx vercel --prod --yes 2>&1 | tail -2
